focus: maintainability across ownership clarity, schema reuse, generated metadata, and change amplification

findingDetails.title: Completed research phases retain pre-run continuity state
findingDetails.evidence: Parent continuity remains 0% and directs phase 001 execution while phases 001-003 are complete; phase-002/003 specs retain 10% pre-run next actions despite complete implementation summaries.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Parent and all ten child lifecycle surfaces were inventoried; stale completed-phase continuity is confined to the parent and phase-002/003 specs.

claimAdjudication.claim: Canonical packet surfaces give obsolete next actions and completion percentages for already-completed research phases.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Checked later status tables, implementation-summary frontmatter, parent phase map, planned child lifecycle fields, and phase-parent graph metadata.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Child resume normally prefers implementation-summary continuity, containing some impact but not removing contradictory canonical completion metadata.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 only if every completion validator and memory/resume consumer excludes spec.md continuity for these packets.

findingDetails.title: Hydration has no contracted filesystem-containment boundary
findingDetails.evidence: The hydration contract has generation guards but no canonical-root, traversal, or symlink acceptance criterion.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Scoped phase-004/006/007/010 searches found no containment control; phase 004 is the shared filesystem producer.

claimAdjudication.claim: The phase-004 contract permits a conforming implementation to hydrate a corpus path that resolves outside the styles root because no containment or symlink invariant is specified.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Searched phase-004/006/007/010 artifacts for realpath, canonicalization, traversal, root confinement, and symlink controls.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Implementation may independently enforce containment, but neither containment nor fixed safe path generation is an acceptance criterion.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if immutable manifest paths, canonical containment, symlink policy, and negative traversal fixtures become required.

findingDetails.title: The STUDY injection envelope records a boundary but does not neutralize untrusted instructions
findingDetails.evidence: The planned transformer injects corpus observations but specifies no data-only parsing, instruction rejection, or behavioral non-influence fixture.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Phase-006 artifacts repeat the envelope but lack prompt-injection behavior criteria; phase-007 authority ordering does not sanitize content.

claimAdjudication.claim: The planned phase-006 controls can pass while corpus-borne instructions influence generated prose, because the envelope is metadata and the leak gate only tests copied values/spans.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Searched planning artifacts for prompt injection, untrusted content, instruction rejection, sanitization, and injection-boundary controls.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: A strict transformer could incidentally discard imperatives, but current criteria do not require that property and leak signals do not detect non-literal instruction following.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the transformer becomes a closed data-only schema with instruction rejection and behavioral fixtures.

findingDetails.title: Canonical parent surfaces omit the implementation-phase dependency graph
findingDetails.evidence: Child packets declare eleven implementation dependency edges, but the parent handoff table and all planned child graph dependency arrays omit them.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Dependency searches covered the parent and every phase-004-010 child planning and graph surface.

claimAdjudication.claim: The canonical parent and graph metadata cannot represent or validate the implementation-phase ordering that child packets require.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Reviewed child dependency tables, blocked tasks, predecessor/successor references, parent transition rules, and all phase-004-010 graph fields.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Child-local blockers encode ordering and limit immediate risk, but do not populate canonical parent or machine-readable graph surfaces.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if graph dependencies are proven unrelated to child sequencing and child blockers are the sole validated authority.

findingDetails.title: Two implementation packets classify existing mode and transport surfaces as new creations
findingDetails.evidence: Phase 009 marks existing foundations and motion contracts as Create in spec and plan; phase 010's spec marks the existing transport directory Create while its plan says Add.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact change-type searches covered phase-004-010 artifacts and direct reads proved all three runtime contracts already exist.

claimAdjudication.claim: The phase-009 and phase-010 owning specs classify three existing runtime surfaces as creations, permitting implementation to replace or independently scaffold contracts that must instead be extended.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Reviewed affected-surface plans, implementation wording, current contracts, and phase-008 modification conventions for evidence that Create meant additive files only.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Phase 009 later says additions and phase 010's plan says Add, reducing destructive intent; the owning action labels remain contradictory and phase 009 does not name the additive file boundary.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if dispatch ignores change labels and an authoritative affected-file manifest proves additive-only edits.

results.summary: Ownership is named, but three existing surfaces are classified as creations.
The shared seam owns common fields once and downstream phases reference or reuse it.
Contradictory create/add labels amplify reconciliation across foundations, motion, and transport.

noveltyJustification: One new P1 (weight 5) was added to five cumulative P1 findings (weight 25); four authoritative findings were carried without refinement credit.
edgeCases: planned implementation limits immediate runtime impact

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Seven planned implementation packets and three existing runtime consumers create a cross-packet maintainability surface requiring strict review-depth evidence.

targetSelection.selectionReason: The assignment names phases 004-010 and parent ownership; current contracts were selected only to verify whether proposed surfaces already exist.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Existing runtime owners are mislabeled as new creation surfaces.

searchActions.result: Phase 009 repeats Create for two existing modes; phase 010 spec says Create while plan says Add.

searchActions.queryOrPath: current foundations, motion, and Open Design SKILL.md contracts
searchActions.result: All three target surfaces already exist and declare current ownership.

searchLedger.rationale: The contradictory creation labels are concrete ownership drift.

searchLedger.invariant: Common proof/handoff fields must have one owner and downstream consumers must reference rather than copy them.

searchActions.result: Phase 007 owns the fields; phases 008/009 consume them and phase-010 T001 explicitly reuses the seam.

searchLedger.rationale: No downstream packet requires a parallel common-field schema.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The producer/consumer contracts consistently preserve phase-007 ownership and reuse.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Contradictory surface actions multiply reconciliation across consumers.

searchActions.result: Three existing consumers require reinterpretation from Create to additive modification; phase-010 documents both labels.

searchLedger.rationale: The same classification defect affects three independently owned consumers.

detail: Authoritative correctness finding preserved with complete typed claim packet; no refinement credit.
Authoritative filesystem-containment finding preserved with complete typed claim packet; no refinement credit.
Authoritative STUDY instruction-neutralization finding preserved with complete typed claim packet; no refinement credit.
Authoritative dependency-projection finding preserved with complete typed claim packet; no refinement credit.
MCP trigger matching timed out; direct reads and exact searches supplied evidence.

reason: Phase 007 owns the common fields and downstream phases explicitly reference or reuse that seam.
